issues.description: Test file uses kebab-case (export-csv.test.ts) while its implementation file uses camelCase (exportCsv.ts). Test file names must match the source file they test.
issues.rule: Test files should be named as `[source-file-name].test.ts`. The source is `exportCsv.ts`, so the test should be `exportCsv.test.ts`.
issues.fix: Rename `export-csv.test.ts` to `exportCsv.test.ts` to match the source file.

issues.description: Test file `SocialIcons.test.tsx` has no matching source file. It actually tests `DiscordIcon.tsx` and `GoogleIcon.tsx`, making the name misleading.
issues.rule: Test files should correspond to the component they test. A test named SocialIcons.test.tsx implies a SocialIcons.tsx component.
issues.fix: Rename to `SocialIconsGroup.test.tsx` or split into `DiscordIcon.test.tsx` and `GoogleIcon.test.tsx` co-located with each source component.

issues.description: Test file `CartDrawerWithItems.test.tsx` has no matching source file. It tests `CartDrawer.tsx` (filled with items scenario) but the name implies a non-existent `CartDrawerWithItems.tsx` component.
issues.rule: Test file names should match the source file they primarily test.
issues.fix: If this is a scenario-based test for CartDrawer, rename to `CartDrawer.withItems.test.tsx` or merge into a shared `CartDrawer.test.tsx`.

issues.description: Test file `HomeSections.test.tsx` has no matching source file `HomeSections.tsx`. The test imports from multiple landing page components (CtaSection, HeroSection, etc.).
issues.rule: Test file names should correspond to the source component or module they test.
issues.fix: Rename to reflect what is actually being tested, e.g. `LandingPage.sections.test.tsx`, or split into per-component test files.

issues.description: Some type files use a PascalCase descriptive name (`GalleryTypes.ts`, `CategoryTypes.ts`, `PaymentMethodTypes.ts`, `SectionItemTypes.ts`) while the predominant convention across the codebase is a plain `types.ts` file inside the appropriate domain folder.
issues.rule: Per naming conventions, type files should be `types.ts` (inside a scoped directory) or follow `UserTypes.ts` pattern when aggregating types outside a domain/ directory. The inconsistency is noticeable since every other feature uses `types.ts`.
issues.fix: Consider standardising all shared type files to `types.ts` within their respective domain/ directories. If a single `types.ts` is too broad, use descriptive camelCase file names (`galleryTypes.ts`, `categoryTypes.ts`) rather than PascalCase to be consistent with utility file naming.

issues.description: A types file lives inside the `presentation/components` layer rather than the `domain/` layer. Type definitions used by multiple components should live in `domain/types/` per Clean Architecture rules.
issues.rule: Types belong in the `domain/` layer. Placing `SectionItemTypes.ts` inside `presentation/components/inline-editor/` violates layer boundaries.
issues.fix: Move to `apps/studio/src/features/products/domain/types/sectionItemTypes.ts` (or merge into `domain/types.ts`) and import from there in the inline-editor components.

issues.description: There are two files named `receiptStorage.ts` in the payments app — one in `shared/infrastructure/` and one in `features/checkout/infrastructure/`. This is a naming collision that can cause confusion about which one is in use.
issues.rule: File names within an app should be sufficiently unique to avoid ambiguity.
issues.fix: Investigate if these are duplicates (DRY violation) or distinct. If distinct, rename the shared one to reflect the broader scope (e.g. `receiptStorageShared.ts` or move and rename to clarify purpose).

issues.description: Context provider files (CartContext.tsx, FlyToCartContext.tsx) live in the `application/` layer directly instead of `application/context/`. Other apps (admin, store shared, studio) consistently use an `application/context/` subfolder for context files.
issues.rule: For consistency, context files should be placed in `application/context/` to match the established pattern across all other apps.
issues.fix: Move `CartContext.tsx` and `FlyToCartContext.tsx` to `apps/store/src/features/cart/application/context/` and update imports accordingly.

patterns.name: Consistent component file naming
patterns.description: All React component files (.tsx) in features/ and shared/presentation/ use PascalCase correctly (e.g. CartDrawer.tsx, ProductCard.tsx, TemplateEditor.tsx). No violations found.

patterns.name: Consistent hook file naming
patterns.description: All custom hook files use camelCase with 'use' prefix (e.g. useProducts.ts, useCartFromCookie.ts, useSupabaseAuth.ts). No violations found.

patterns.name: Consistent constant naming
patterns.description: Exported global constants across domain/constants.ts files use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE correctly (e.g. USERS_PER_PAGE, CHECKOUT_COMPLETED_SESSION_KEY). Local variables use camelCase. No violations found.

patterns.name: Consistent folder naming
patterns.description: Feature folders use kebab-case (seller-admins, payment-methods, received-orders, product-detail). Layer folders use lowercase (domain, application, infrastructure, presentation). No PascalCase folders found.

patterns.name: Next.js reserved file names
patterns.description: Next.js special files (page.tsx, layout.tsx, error.tsx, global-error.tsx, not-found.tsx, providers.tsx) all correctly use lowercase as required by the framework. These are intentional exceptions to PascalCase component naming.

patterns.name: E2E test file naming
patterns.description: E2E spec files in e2e/ directories use kebab-case (auth-session.spec.ts, permission-management.spec.ts). This is an accepted convention for Playwright test files and is consistent across all apps.
